get.description: Returns a list of all the open issue groups in your account, as seen in The Feed

parameters.name: filter_code_repo_id
parameters.description: The repository ID to filter the issue groups by

parameters.name: filter_external_code_repo_id
parameters.description: The repository ID from the provider to filter the issue groups by.

parameters.name: filter_code_repo_name
parameters.description: The repository name to filter the issue groups by.

parameters.name: filter_container_repo_id
parameters.description: The container repository to filter the issue groups by.

schema.description: Unique ID of the container repository

parameters.name: filter_team_id
parameters.description: Filter issue groups for a specific team

parameters.name: filter_issue_type
parameters.description: A filter to only export issues that have a given type

200.description: A list of open issue groups, sorted descending by their priority

id.description: The issue group ID.

title.description: A human readable title for this issue group.

description.description: A small description of this issue.

type.description: The type of issues in this group.

severity_score.description: A number indicating the importance of the vulnerability

severity.description: A human friendly version of the issue's importance

group_status.description: The current status of the issue group.

time_to_fix_minutes.description: The estimated time in minutes that it will take to fix this issue

locations.description: A list of locations where this issue was found. A vulnerability can be found in multiple types of locations eg: when a container is linked to a code repository, any vulnerabilities found in that container, will also have that code repository listed as location.

items.title: Location
items.description: An object describing where a vulnerability was found.

id.description: The ID of the location in Aikido.

name.description: The name of the location.

example.title: firefox-esr
example.description: Attacker can trigger memory corruption leading to crash or remote code execution

get.description: Returns a list of all issues (open, ignored, snoozed, closed,..) in your account in JSON or CSV format

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The format of the export

parameters.name: filter_status
parameters.description: A filter to only export issues that have a given status.

parameters.name: filter_team_id
parameters.description: A filter to only export issues that belong to a given team

schema.description: Unique ID of the team

parameters.name: filter_issue_group_id
parameters.description: A filter to only export issues that belong to a given issue group

schema.description: Unique ID of the issue group

parameters.name: filter_code_repo_id
parameters.description: A filter to only export issues that belong to a given code repo

schema.description: Unique ID of the code repository

parameters.name: filter_code_repo_name
parameters.description: A filter to only export issues that belong to a code repo which matches this name exactly

schema.description: The name of the code repo

parameters.name: filter_container_repo_id
parameters.description: A filter to only export issues that belong to a given container repo

schema.description: Unique ID of the container repository

parameters.name: filter_container_repo_name
parameters.description: A filter to only export issues that belong to a container repo which matches this name exactly

schema.description: The name of the container

parameters.name: filter_container_repo_ids
parameters.description: A filter to only export issues that belong to given container repos (up to 15). Multiple values should be comma separated. Only available for Pro and Scale workspaces.

schema.description: A string of comma separated unique ID's of the container repositories

parameters.name: filter_domain_id
parameters.description: A filter to only export issues that belong to a given domain

schema.description: Unique ID of the domain

parameters.name: filter_virtual_machine_id
parameters.description: A filter to only export issues that belong to a given virtual machine

schema.description: Unique ID of the virtual machine

parameters.name: filter_issue_type
parameters.description: A filter to only export issues that have a give type

parameters.name: filter_severities
parameters.description: A filter to only export issues with the specified severities. Multiple values should be comma separated.

parameters.name: sla_remediate_by_after
parameters.description: A filter to only export issues with a sla_remediate_by timestamp after the specified unix timestamp (in seconds).

parameters.name: sla_remediate_by_before
parameters.description: A filter to only export issues with a sla_remediate_by timestamp before the specified unix timestamp (in seconds).

parameters.name: filter_language
parameters.description: A filter to only export issues of the specified language.

200.description: A list of issues, sorted descending by their priority in the requested format

id.description: The issue ID.

type.description: The type of issues in this group.

rule.description: The name of the rule that caused this finding (eg 'SQL injection')

rule_id.description: The id of the rule that caused this finding (eg 'aik_cloud_aws_001')

attack_surface.description: The attack surface of this issue.

severity_score.description: A number indicating the importance of the vulnerability

original_cvss_severity_score.description: A number indicating the importance of the vulnerability based on the original CVSS score

severity.description: A human friendly version of the issue's importance

status.description: The current status of the issue.

affected_package.description: The name of affected open-source package, if any

affected_file.description: The name of affected file, if any

cve_id.description: CVE ID in case of an issue of type open_source

first_detected_at.description: A timestamp indicating when the issue was first created

code_repo_id.description: The id of the code repository where this issue was found

code_repo_name.description: The name of code repository where this issue was found

cloud_id.description: The id of the cloud where this issue was found

cloud_name.description: The name of the cloud where this issue was found

container_repo_id.description: The id of the container image where this issue was found

container_repo_name.description: The name of the container image where this issue was found

domain_id.description: The id of the domain where this issue was found

domain_name.description: The name of the domain where this issue was found

sla_days.description: The amount of days wherein this issue should be fixed following the configured SLA for this severity

sla_remediate_by.description: An epoch timestamp in seconds by when this issue should be fixed following the configured SLA for this severity

ignored_at.description: A timestamp indicating when the issue was ignored. This value will be 'null' if it is not ignored.

ignored_by.description: A string indicating the way this issue got ignored. Can be one of the following: 'auto', 'user', 'rule', 'api'.

closed_at.description: A timestamp indicating when the issue was closed. This value will be 'null' if it is not closed.

start_line.description: A number indicating the line number of the file where the affected code of the issue begins. This value will be 'null' when the issue is not a sast or secret issue.

end_line.description: A number indicating the line number of the file where the affected code of the issue ends. This value will be 'null' when the issue is not a sast or secret issue.

snooze_until.description: A timestamp indicating until when the issue will be snoozed. This value will be 'null' if the issue not snoozed.

cwe_classes.description: A list of CWE classes that apply to this issue

installed_version.description: The current version of the package related to this issue. This is only applicable for issues of type 'open_source'.

patched_versions.description: A list of versions that contain a fix for this issue. This is only applicable for issues of type 'open_source'.

license_type.description: The license type related to this issue. This is only applicable for issues of type 'license'.

programming_language.description: The programming language related to this issue. This is only applicable for issues of type 'open_source' and 'sast'.

type.description: The type of issues in this group.

rule.description: The name of the rule that caused this finding (eg 'SQL injection')

attack_surface.description: The attack surface of this issue.

severity_score.description: A number indicating the importance of the vulnerability

severity.description: A human friendly version of the issue's importance

status.description: The current status of the issue.

affected_package.description: The name of affected open-source package, if any

affected_file.description: The name of affected file, if any

cve_id.description: The CVE ID when it is an open_source issue

first_detected_at.description: A timestamp indicating when the issue was first created

code_repo_id.description: The id of the code repository where this issue was found

code_repo_name.description: The name of code repository where this issue was found

cloud_id.description: The id of the cloud where this issue was found

cloud_name.description: The name of the cloud where this issue was found

container_repo_id.description: The id of the container image where this issue was found

container_repo_name.description: The name of the container image where this issue was found

virtual_machine_id.description: The id of the cloud instance where this issue was found

virtual_machine_name.description: The name of the cloud instance where this issue was found

domain_id.description: The id of the domain where this issue was found

domain_name.description: The name of the domain where this issue was found

sla_remediate_by.description: An epoch timestamp in seconds by when this issue should be fixed following the configured SLA for this severity

ignored_at.description: A timestamp indicating when the issue was ignored. This value will be 'null' if it is not ignored.

closed_at.description: A timestamp indicating when the issue was closed. This value will be 'null' if it is not closed.

issue_type_metadata.description: An object containing additional information about the issue by the issue type. This value will not always be set.

reachability_status.description: The current status of the issue group.

snooze_until.description: A timestamp indicating until when the issue will be snoozed. This value will be 'null' if the issue is not snoozed.
